title: IPv4 Address Structure and Notation
description: A visual breakdown of the 32-bit IPv4 address structure, showing its division into octets, binary representation, and conversion to dotted-decimal notation.
relationships: The 32-bit address is split into 4 octets
Each octet is converted from binary to a decimal number
The decimal values are separated by dots to form the human-readable address
A subnet mask determines the division between the network and host portions
